extraction.one_line_summary: HAAS outsprinted VAN AVERMAART from a 14-rider selection that crested the final climb with 5km to go, catching him wrong-footed in final kilometer.
tactical_patterns: BMC dominated chase and leadout for VAN AVERMAART but HAAS still beat him, suggesting Olympic champion predictable in positioning.
Summit selection with 5km remaining created elite sprint group, rewarding GC-capable fast finishers over pure sprinters.
ASTANA pacing on climbs fractured peloton before BMC took over, showing shared interest in hard selective finish.
IRIZAR as oldest rider targeted breakaway on multiple climbs, revealing veteran calculation about energy expenditure versus selection risk.

rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: positive: Studied positioning to exploit VAN AVERMAART's predictable leadout, shows tactical homework beats raw power in selective sprints
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAART Greg: neutral: Had perfect BMC leadout but still lost, may be vulnerable when opponents scout his finishing patterns closely
rider_signals.PATE Danny: negative: Former U23 TT champion dropped early in breakaway on climbs, suggests poor climbing form
rider_signals.IRIZAR Markel: positive: At 37, lasted longest in break by pacing climbs intelligently before final catch
